title: Al-Quran-ul-Karim
gloss: The Quran with translation in Urdu.
notes: Complete Urdu Quran; four-part pagination; two-tier pricing.

title: Qurrat-ul-Waizin
gloss: A book of sermons translated from the original in Arabic by Shaikh Usman bin Husain Khobvi.
copyright: Malak Ghulam Muhammad, Book-seller, Lahore; No. 123, dated the 6th August, 1910.

title: Mujmua-i-Khutab-i-Mutarjam
gloss: For subject see entry No. 15 at page 39 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1910.
notes: Dual date: 1328 Hijri / June 1910. Cross-reference: 1910Q2 entry 15, page 39 (our Q2 extraction). [Previous edition noticed in entry No 15 on page 39 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1910.]

author: Muhammad Yaqub, Maulvi
title: Masala-i-Jawaz-i-Sihra
gloss: A booklet in which the writer tries to prove that wearing of chaplets by the bridegroom on the occasion of marriage is allowable by the Muhammadan law.
notes: Fiqh defense of the wedding sehra (chaplet) custom.

flags.issue: last word smudged — read as Sihra (chaplet), matching the gloss

title: Tafsir-ul-Quran, Jild-i-Awwal
gloss: The first two Suras or verses of the Quran entitled the 'Al-Hamd' and the 'Baqr' with translation and commentary by Maulavi Muhammad Insha Ullah, Editor, the "Watan," Lahore.
copyright: M. Muhammad Insha Ullah, Lahore; No. 111, dated the 5th September, 1910.
notes: Three-volume Quran commentary (serials 25–27) by the editor of the newspaper Watan — journalist as exegete.

title: Tafsir-ul-Quran, Jild Duwam
gloss: The third Sura or verse entitled the Al-i-Imran which extends over chapters 3rd, 4th and 5th of the Quran, with commentary by Muhammad Insha Ullah Khan.
publisher: The Commentator, Proprietor and Editor of the "Watan"
copyright: M. Muhammad Insha Ullah, Lahore; No. 112, dated the 5th September, 1910.

title: Tafsir-ul-Quran, Jild Siwum
gloss: The fourth Sura or verse of the Quran entitled the 'Nisa' or 'Women' with translation and commentary by Maulvi Muhammad Insha Ullah, volume III.
publisher: The commentator who is editor of the "Watan"
copyright: M. Muhammad Insha Ullah, Lahore; No. 117, dated the 6th August, 1910.
